senses.gloss: that has died by drowning; drowned

examples.text:
10th century, Al-Mutanabbi
وَٱلْهَجْرُ أَقْتَلُ لِي مِمَّا أُرَاقِبُهُ / أَنَا الْغَرِيقُ فَمَا خَوْفِي مِنَ الْبَلَلِ
wal-hajru ʔaqtalu lī mimmā ʔurāqibuhu / ʔanā al-ḡarīqu famā ḵawfī mina l-balali
examples.english: (please add an English translation of this quotation)

senses.gloss: someone who has died by drowning

etymology.text: فَعِيل (faʕīl)-type stative-verb derived from the active participle from the verb غَرَقَ (ḡaraqa, “to drown”).
